title: Gastrolepis austrocaledonica
taxonomy: Gastrolepis austrocaledonica is classified as a species of flowering plant in the family Polypodiaceae, a family of ferns commonly known as the polypody ferns. It belongs to the genus Gastrolepis, which is a group of small to medium-sized ferns primarily found in the Pacific region. The species is further classified as a type of epiphytic fern, meaning it grows on other plants, such as trees, for support. Gastrolepis austrocaledonica is native to the islands of New Caledonia in the Pacific Ocean and is often found growing in humid, shaded areas. Within the Polypodiaceae family, Gastrolepis austrocaledonica is considered to be a rare species.
uses: Gastrolepis austrocaledonica is a plant native to New Caledonia, but its medicinal uses are not well-documented.
